fullPlan.projectDescription: Migrate the production application database from PostgreSQL 14 to CockroachDB (cloud-hosted) with zero downtime and zero data loss during the cutover. The application uses Prisma ORM across ~40 tables totaling ~2TB, with moderate write throughput (peak 5k writes/sec during business hours). Critical paths include user authentication, billing transactions, and audit log append. Business requirement: complete migration within 6 weeks of project start, with live cutover executed during a 2-hour scheduled maintenance window. Rollback must be possible for 30 days post-cutover. No raw SQL — all queries through Prisma client. Staging uses CockroachDB Cloud Serverless; production uses Dedicated tier.
nonGoals: Do not change the Prisma schema semantics — client-side code must compile and pass tests unchanged
Do not remove PostgreSQL until 30-day post-cutover stability window passes
Do not migrate denormalized read-replica data or analytics pipelines — those are a separate post-cutover project
Do not introduce multi-region writes — single-region CockroachDB only (multi-region is future scope)
Do not disable any existing monitoring, alerting, or logging paths during migration
Do not increase p99 application latency by more than 15% vs PostgreSQL baseline measured over the migration week
Do not modify business logic or application behavior — this is a pure storage-layer migration
Do not execute Postgres decommission — that happens after the 30-day stability window, which is outside this project's scope
Do not treat read-replica or analytics data as in scope for consistency validation

phases.title: Schema Compatibility Audit
phases.description: Audit all 40 tables for CockroachDB incompatibilities and produce a migration checklist. This phase gates every schema and application change that follows — nothing can be scoped without knowing what needs adapting.
acceptanceCriteria: All 40 tables catalogued with any CockroachDB-incompatible types flagged (SERIAL, sequences, arrays, PostGIS, pg-specific extensions)
FK dependency graph documented with audit log's 12 FK dependencies explicitly ordered for safe schema migration
PII-containing tables (users, payments and any others) enumerated with encryption-at-rest requirements noted
CockroachDB-incompatible DDL constructs listed with required substitution for each
Migration checklist artifact committed, covering schema changes and known behavioral differences

executorDecides: Format of the migration checklist artifact
Which secondary audit tooling to use (pganalyze, manual inspection, CockroachDB schema import dry-run)
escalateToOperator: Any table using a Postgres feature with no CockroachDB equivalent that would require application behavior changes
Any PII table with ambiguous data classification requiring compliance review

phases.title: Staging Environment Setup + PostgreSQL Baseline Capture
phases.description: Provision CockroachDB Cloud Serverless for staging and capture PostgreSQL p99 latency and throughput baselines. Both outputs are hard dependencies for later phases — the staging instance for all validation work, the baseline for the ≤15% latency non-goal.
acceptanceCriteria: CockroachDB Cloud Serverless staging cluster provisioned with credentials and network access configured
Application can establish a Prisma connection to CockroachDB staging from the test environment
PostgreSQL p99 latency baseline captured for auth, billing, and audit log critical paths under representative load
PostgreSQL write throughput baseline captured at or above peak 5k writes/sec
Baseline metrics artifact committed for reference in P10

executorDecides: Benchmarking tool choice (k6, pgbench, custom script)
Staging cluster sizing within Serverless tier
Secrets management approach for staging credentials
escalateToOperator: Network peering or VPC requirements that need infra team involvement
Compliance requirements on staging data (whether production PII can be used)

phases.title: Prisma Schema Adaptation for CockroachDB
phases.description: Adapt prisma.schema to target CockroachDB while preserving the generated client API exactly. This is the most likely source of non-goal-1 violations and must be validated by running the existing test suite unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma.schema uses cockroachdb provider and resolves all incompatibilities identified in p1 (SERIAL → UUID/cuid, sequences, etc.)
prisma generate succeeds without errors or warnings against the adapted schema
All existing application test files compile without modification
Existing test suite passes against the adapted schema on a local CockroachDB-compatible target
Audit log table and its 12 FK-referenced tables represented correctly in adapted schema

executorDecides: UUID generation strategy (cuid, uuid, nanoid) for tables that used SERIAL PKs
Whether to use prisma migrate or prisma db push for staging deployment
Schema file organization (single file vs. multi-file if supported)
escalateToOperator: Any schema change that would alter the generated Prisma client types exposed to application code
Any PK strategy change that would break existing FK references or application-layer ID assumptions

phases.title: Transaction Semantics Audit + Application Compatibility Fixes
phases.description: Identify and fix all application code paths affected by CockroachDB's serializable-by-default isolation and behavioral differences from PostgreSQL. Unaddressed serialization conflicts will surface as silent data errors or panics in production.
acceptanceCriteria: All code paths using advisory locks identified; each either ported to an application-level alternative or confirmed unsupported and escalated
All Prisma transaction blocks audited for serialization conflict exposure; retry logic (handling error code 40001) added where required
All Postgres-specific error code references in application error handlers updated or aliased for CockroachDB equivalents
Deferred constraint usage (if any) identified and resolved — CockroachDB does not support deferred FK constraints
Retry layer unit-tested: confirms retries occur on 40001, does not retry on other error classes

executorDecides: Retry backoff strategy (exponential, fixed, jitter)
Maximum retry count and timeout per transaction class
Whether to implement retry at Prisma middleware layer or per-call site
escalateToOperator: Any advisory lock usage that cannot be replaced without changing application behavior
Any deferred constraint that enforces a business rule — removal may require product sign-off

phases.title: Data Migration Tooling Setup + Throughput Validation
phases.description: Select, configure, and validate tooling for the bulk initial data load and ongoing consistency checking. The 2TB/2-hour cutover requirement is a hard constraint — tooling that can't hit that throughput invalidates the cutover plan.
acceptanceCriteria: Bulk load tooling selected and configured (IMPORT INTO via pg_dump export, or equivalent) against staging CockroachDB
Throughput benchmark confirms 2TB load completes within 120 minutes under staging conditions, or bottleneck is documented and escalated
Reconciliation tooling operational: row-count and checksum comparison between Postgres and CockroachDB for any table
Dual-write consistency checker operational: samples a configurable percentage of write pairs and compares resulting reads from both DBs
PII table data validation script operational: validates row counts, field presence, and encryption-at-rest flag for users and payments tables

executorDecides: Bulk load tool selection (IMPORT INTO, COPY, custom chunked upsert)
Reconciliation sampling rate and comparison granularity
Checksum algorithm choice
escalateToOperator: If 2TB/2hr throughput cannot be achieved — may require window extension or phased table cutover instead of atomic
If encryption-at-rest verification requires CockroachDB Cloud console access not yet provisioned

phases.title: Staging Schema Deployment
phases.description: Apply the validated Prisma schema to CockroachDB staging in FK-dependency order. This is the first end-to-end proof that the adapted schema is deployable — a failing migration here blocks all data work.
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma migrations execute successfully against CockroachDB staging with no manual SQL intervention
All 40 tables present with correct column types, constraints, and indexes
Audit log FK constraints satisfied: all 12 referenced tables exist and have PKs at migration time
Migration is idempotent: re-running does not error or duplicate data
Schema diff between Postgres production and CockroachDB staging documented — all differences intentional and explained

executorDecides: Migration execution order for FK-constrained tables
Whether to use a single migration file or ordered set
escalateToOperator: Any schema difference that is not a known CockroachDB adaptation and cannot be explained by p1 findings

phases.title: Staging Data Load + PII and Integrity Validation
phases.description: Load a production data snapshot into CockroachDB staging and validate correctness. This is the first real test that the tooling from p5 works against actual production data shape and volume.
acceptanceCriteria: Production data snapshot loaded into all 40 CockroachDB staging tables
Row counts match between Postgres source snapshot and CockroachDB staging for all tables
FK integrity validated: no orphaned FK references across all 40 tables post-load
PII validation passed for users and payments tables: row counts match, all required fields present, encryption-at-rest verified active
Billing transactions table validated: transaction count and total sum match source snapshot exactly

executorDecides: Whether to use a full production snapshot or a time-bounded subset for staging
Checksum comparison granularity (table-level vs. row-level sampling)
escalateToOperator: Any row count mismatch not explained by snapshot timing
Any PII validation failure — must not proceed to p8 until resolved
Any billing sum mismatch, however small

phases.title: Dual-Write Infrastructure
phases.description: Build the dual-write layer that fans application writes to both Postgres and CockroachDB simultaneously. This is the technical foundation for zero-downtime cutover — Postgres remains primary until cutover, CockroachDB receives shadow writes.
acceptanceCriteria: Dual-write middleware intercepts all Prisma write operations and executes them against both Postgres and CockroachDB
Feature flag enables/disables dual-write without application redeploy
Billing transaction writes are atomic across both DBs: if CockroachDB write fails, the overall transaction is failed (not silently skipped)
Dual-write failures surface to existing monitoring and alerting paths — no silent failure mode
Consistency checker from p5 is wired to run continuously against dual-write output and emit alerts on detected drift

executorDecides: Whether dual-write is implemented as Prisma middleware, a service layer wrapper, or a proxy
Failure handling strategy for non-billing tables (fail-open vs. fail-closed)
Feature flag mechanism (env var, config service, or database-backed flag)
escalateToOperator: Any dual-write architecture that would require modifying business logic code paths (violates non-goal 7)
Decision to make non-billing dual-write failures fail-open — requires explicit operator risk acceptance

phases.title: Staging Dual-Write Validation Under Load
phases.description: Enable dual-write in staging and validate correctness and consistency under peak load. This is the primary integration test of the entire migration stack before any production work begins.
acceptanceCriteria: Dual-write runs continuously in staging under sustained 5k writes/sec load with zero consistency checker alerts over a 4-hour test window
Billing transactions: zero data loss confirmed — every write to Postgres confirmed present in CockroachDB post-test
Audit log append ordering consistent between Postgres and CockroachDB under concurrent write load
Retry layer exercises correctly: serialization conflicts resolved by retry, not surfaced as application errors
Dual-write adds no more than 30% latency overhead to write operations (absolute increase, not the 15% non-goal which applies to post-cutover reads)

executorDecides: Load test tool and traffic shape
Consistency check sampling rate during load test
Whether to run auth, billing, and audit as separate load test scenarios or combined
escalateToOperator: Any consistency drift detected during load test — must not proceed to p10 until root cause identified and fixed
Any billing data loss, however small
Dual-write write latency overhead exceeding 30% — may require architectural reconsideration

phases.title: Performance Benchmarking Against ≤15% Latency Target
phases.description: Measure CockroachDB p99 latency on read-path critical operations against the PostgreSQL baseline from p2 and validate the ≤15% non-goal. This must pass before production provisioning is approved — a latency violation here requires tuning, not workarounds.
acceptanceCriteria: p99 read latency on auth critical path ≤ 115% of Postgres baseline under production-like load
p99 read latency on billing critical path ≤ 115% of Postgres baseline
p99 read latency on audit log read path ≤ 115% of Postgres baseline
Any index or connection pool tuning applied to hit latency targets is documented with before/after measurements
Benchmark results artifact committed for operator review before p12 begins

executorDecides: Index additions to CockroachDB that are not in Postgres schema (query-plan-driven tuning)
Connection pool size adjustments
Query hint usage if CockroachDB query planner diverges from Postgres
escalateToOperator: Any latency target breach that cannot be resolved by index or connection pool tuning — may require scope review or window extension
Any tuning that would require schema changes affecting Prisma client types

phases.title: Rollback Procedure Development + End-to-End Drill
phases.description: Write the complete rollback runbook and execute it end-to-end in staging. A rollback plan that has never been executed is not a rollback plan — the 30-day post-cutover rollback requirement demands demonstrated capability.
acceptanceCriteria: Rollback runbook covers two scenarios: cutover abort (never switched) and post-cutover rollback (switched, then rolled back within 30 days)
Rollback trigger criteria defined: specific error rate thresholds, data drift thresholds, and latency breach values that mandate rollback
End-to-end rollback drill executed in staging: CockroachDB activated as primary, rollback trigger simulated, Postgres restored as primary, data consistency verified
Postgres data consistency after rollback drill confirmed: no writes lost that occurred after CockroachDB was made primary during the drill
Runbook timing validated: rollback completes within a defined time bound documented in the runbook

executorDecides: Specific error rate thresholds for rollback triggers (within reasonable bounds)
Runbook format and tooling (manual steps vs. scripted automation)
escalateToOperator: Rollback trigger thresholds that could cause false positives under normal traffic fluctuation — operator must accept final values
Any rollback scenario that cannot be executed within the maintenance window

phases.title: Production CockroachDB Provisioning
phases.description: Provision the CockroachDB Dedicated production cluster with security and network configuration. This is the only phase that touches production infrastructure before the data load — it must be gated on staging validation passing.
acceptanceCriteria: CockroachDB Dedicated tier production cluster provisioned in correct cloud region
Encryption-at-rest enabled and verified on the production cluster
Network peering or private connectivity from production application to CockroachDB Dedicated established
Prisma connection string for production CockroachDB validated: application can connect and execute a DDL smoke test
IAM and credential access controls documented and match security requirements for PII data

executorDecides: Cluster sizing (node count, CPU/RAM) within Dedicated tier based on p10 benchmarks
Backup schedule configuration on the CockroachDB Dedicated cluster
escalateToOperator: Any networking topology that requires changes to production firewall rules or VPC peering agreements
Cluster sizing decisions that significantly exceed budget — operator must approve spend

phases.title: Production Schema Deployment
phases.description: Apply the staging-validated schema to production CockroachDB in FK-dependency order. Uses the identical migration artifacts from p6, not a re-derivation — this is a promotion, not a re-run.
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma migration artifacts from p6 applied to production CockroachDB without modification
All 40 tables present in production CockroachDB with correct types, constraints, and indexes
Audit log FK constraints satisfied in production schema
Migration idempotency confirmed: re-run does not error
Schema diff between production Postgres and production CockroachDB matches the diff documented in p6

executorDecides: Whether to run migration via CI pipeline or direct CLI invocation
escalateToOperator: Any migration failure in production — must not proceed to p14 until fully resolved and root cause understood

phases.title: Production Initial Data Load + PII and Integrity Validation
phases.description: Load a production Postgres snapshot into CockroachDB production and run the full validation suite. This is the highest-stakes data operation in the project — billing zero-loss and PII validation are hard gates.
acceptanceCriteria: Full production data snapshot loaded into all 40 CockroachDB production tables within the throughput window validated in p5
Row counts match between Postgres snapshot and CockroachDB production for all 40 tables
FK integrity validated: no orphaned references across all tables
PII validation passed: users and payments tables row counts match, encryption-at-rest confirmed active on production cluster
Billing transactions sum and count match source snapshot exactly — zero tolerance for discrepancy

executorDecides: Snapshot timing relative to business hours to minimize data age at load start
Whether to pause and re-snapshot if load duration would make data more than N hours stale
escalateToOperator: Any row count mismatch — must not proceed to p15
Any PII validation failure
Any billing discrepancy, however small
Load throughput falling below the validated rate, risking the 2-hour cutover window

phases.title: Production Dual-Write Activation
phases.description: Enable the dual-write feature flag in production with Postgres remaining the read/write primary. This begins the period where CockroachDB receives every production write — the first real production validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Dual-write feature flag enabled in production without application redeploy
All production write paths confirmed reaching CockroachDB via dual-write layer monitoring
No increase in application error rate above baseline during first 30 minutes post-activation
p99 write latency within 30% of Postgres-only baseline during first 30 minutes (dual-write overhead acceptable per p9)
Consistency checker running against production dual-write output with alerts wired to existing monitoring

executorDecides: Ramp-up strategy (immediate full enable vs. gradual percentage rollout via feature flag)
Monitoring window duration before declaring activation stable
escalateToOperator: Any error rate increase during activation — must roll back flag immediately
Any consistency alert within first hour of activation

phases.title: Production Dual-Write Validation Period
phases.description: Run dual-write in production for a minimum validation period before cutover rehearsal. This is the sustained evidence baseline — cutover cannot proceed until consistency and latency targets are met over real production load.
acceptanceCriteria: Dual-write runs for minimum 5 business days under full production load with zero consistency alerts
Daily reconciliation reports show zero row count drift across all 40 tables
Billing transaction counts and sums match between Postgres and CockroachDB at each daily reconciliation
p99 read latency on Postgres (current primary) shows no regression vs. baseline — dual-write adds no read overhead
Validation period summary report produced and committed for operator review before p17 is authorized

executorDecides: Reconciliation check scheduling (time of day, frequency)
Report format for the validation summary
escalateToOperator: Any consistency drift detected during validation period — must resolve before advancing
Decision to shorten validation period below 5 business days — operator must explicitly accept reduced evidence
Any billing discrepancy at any reconciliation

phases.title: Cutover Rehearsal in Staging
phases.description: Execute a full 2-hour cutover rehearsal in staging using the production runbook verbatim. This validates the runbook timing, smoke test completeness, and rollback capability within a single window before production execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Full cutover executed in staging within 120 minutes using the production runbook with no ad-hoc steps
All runbook steps timed individually — total time and critical-path time documented
Post-cutover smoke tests (auth login, billing transaction, audit log write/read) pass within the window
Rollback drill executed in same window: CockroachDB made primary, then rolled back to Postgres, data integrity confirmed
Any runbook gaps found during rehearsal resolved and runbook updated before p18

executorDecides: Specific smoke test queries used to validate post-cutover state
Staging data freshness requirements for the rehearsal
escalateToOperator: Any rehearsal step that cannot be completed within the 120-minute window — requires window extension or step elimination decision
Any rollback failure during the drill

phases.title: Production Cutover
phases.description: Execute the 2-hour scheduled maintenance window: drain dual-write, complete final sync, switch all reads and writes to CockroachDB, and verify. PostgreSQL transitions to read-only standby for the rollback window.
acceptanceCriteria: Final Postgres-to-CockroachDB sync completes with zero row count drift before read switch
All application reads and writes routed to CockroachDB within the 120-minute window
Post-cutover smoke tests pass: auth, billing transaction write+read, audit log append+query
CockroachDB p99 latency within 15% of Postgres baseline during first 30 minutes post-switch
PostgreSQL placed in read-only mode and confirmed receiving zero application writes post-cutover
Rollback runbook on standby with operator and on-call team briefed on trigger criteria

executorDecides: Exact step ordering within the runbook (already validated in p17)
Smoke test retry count before declaring failure
escalateToOperator: Any smoke test failure post-switch — operator decides rollback vs. investigate
Any latency breach above 15% in first 30 minutes — operator decides rollback vs. monitor
Window overrun: if cutover cannot complete within 120 minutes, operator must decide extend or abort

phases.title: Post-Cutover Stability Monitoring + Rollback Readiness
phases.description: Monitor production CockroachDB for the 30-day stability window while maintaining Postgres as a verified rollback target. This phase ends at the 30-day mark with a stability report and Postgres decommission criteria documented for the follow-on project.
acceptanceCriteria: CockroachDB production monitored continuously for 30 days with no sustained latency breach (p99 > 115% of baseline for more than 1 hour)
Weekly Postgres consistency spot-checks confirm Postgres data snapshot remains consistent and accessible for rollback
Rollback capability tested by read-only validation query against Postgres weekly — Postgres confirmed rollback-ready throughout window
Any rollback trigger event during the 30-day window documented with root cause and resolution
End-of-window stability report produced documenting: incident count, latency trend, data drift events, and recommended Postgres decommission criteria for the follow-on project

executorDecides: Monitoring dashboard layout and alert grouping
Spot-check query selection for Postgres rollback validation
Stability report format
escalateToOperator: Any rollback trigger event — operator decides whether to roll back or accept the risk
Any sustained Postgres data drift detected during spot-checks (would indicate dual-write inconsistency that persisted through cutover)

fullPlan.decompositionRationale: The project decomposes along four structural seams: (1) staging-first validation — every risky operation is proven in staging before touching production, which forces a staging-then-production doubling of schema, data load, and dual-write phases; (2) the dual-write period as the central safety mechanism — it is its own infrastructure phase (p8) rather than bundled into cutover, because it must run long enough to generate the consistency evidence that authorizes cutover; (3) hard gates between phases — p10 latency benchmarks gate production provisioning, p11 rollback drill gates production dual-write, p16 validation report gates rehearsal, and p17 rehearsal gates the live cutover; and (4) the 30-day rollback window as a first-class phase (p19) rather than an epilogue, because it requires active operational discipline and produces the decommission criteria for the follow-on project. The 19-phase count sits at the low end of the expected 18-28 range because several natural phase boundaries were intentionally merged: PII validation was kept inside its respective data-load phases (p7, p14) rather than extracted as a separate phase, since it executes at the same time and against the same data; staging and production schema deployments are separate phases because they have genuinely different gates and accept different risk levels, but they share artifacts rather than re-deriving them. The 6-week timeline is achievable because p1, p2 are fully parallel; p3, p4, p5 run largely in parallel after p1/p2; and the production phases (p12 onwards) can begin as soon as staging validation clears, overlapping with the tail of p11.
